verses.text: Paul, an apostle, to 'Timothy, my own son in the faith'; grace, mercy, and peace. A father's greeting. He writes to his trusted disciple.
Paul, an apostle, to 'Timothy, my own son in the faith'; grace, mercy, and peace. A father's greeting. He writes to his trusted disciple.
Charge some to 'teach no other doctrine,' nor heed fables and endless genealogies. Guard the truth. Sound teaching, not idle speculation, is to be kept.
Charge some to 'teach no other doctrine,' nor heed fables and endless genealogies. Guard the truth. Sound teaching, not idle speculation, is to be kept.
The end of the charge is 'charity out of a pure heart'; some, wanting to teach, understand not. Love the aim. The goal of all instruction is love.
The end of the charge is 'charity out of a pure heart'; some, wanting to teach, understand not. Love the aim. The goal of all instruction is love.
The end of the charge is 'charity out of a pure heart'; some, wanting to teach, understand not. Love the aim. The goal of all instruction is love.
The law is good 'if a man use it lawfully'; it is made for the lawless. Law's use. It restrains and convicts the ungodly.
The law is good 'if a man use it lawfully'; it is made for the lawless. Law's use. It restrains and convicts the ungodly.
The law is good 'if a man use it lawfully'; it is made for the lawless. Law's use. It restrains and convicts the ungodly.
The law is good 'if a man use it lawfully'; it is made for the lawless. Law's use. It restrains and convicts the ungodly.
I thank Christ, who counted me faithful, though once a blasphemer; His grace 'was exceeding abundant.' Mercy to the chief. Grace reached even the persecutor.
I thank Christ, who counted me faithful, though once a blasphemer; His grace 'was exceeding abundant.' Mercy to the chief. Grace reached even the persecutor.
I thank Christ, who counted me faithful, though once a blasphemer; His grace 'was exceeding abundant.' Mercy to the chief. Grace reached even the persecutor.
'This is a faithful saying... that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.' The gospel. Christ came to save the worst of sinners.
'This is a faithful saying... that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.' The gospel. Christ came to save the worst of sinners.
'Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever.' Doxology. Grace overflows into praise.
War a good warfare, holding 'faith, and a good conscience.' Fight on. The Christian life is a battle to be waged in faith.
War a good warfare, holding 'faith, and a good conscience.' Fight on. The Christian life is a battle to be waged in faith.
